payload.message: Run the session-review and workspace-improvement skills. Review sessions from the last 24 hours, identify failures and knowledge gaps, open PRs for improvements (max 2). Then distill any recurring corrections or new gotchas from today's daily log into MEMORY.md (Hard Lessons or Known Gotchas). If any feedback applies broadly, add it to shared-context/FEEDBACK-LOG.md. Prune daily logs older than 7 days.
Run flux-ops skill to check kustomization health across all 3 clusters (ottawa, robbinsdale, stpetersburg). Report any reconciliation failures or stale revisions.

Run cluster-health skill across all 3 clusters. Check for pods in non-Running state, nodes with pressure conditions, Ceph health warnings, PVCs near capacity, and certificates expiring within 14 days. Summarize findings and alert on anything critical.

Deep memory review. Read all daily logs from the past 7 days. Identify: (1) corrections given more than once — distill into MEMORY.md Hard Lessons, (2) new operational patterns worth preserving — add to MEMORY.md, (3) feedback that applies broadly — add to shared-context/FEEDBACK-LOG.md, (4) stale entries in MEMORY.md that are no longer accurate — remove or update them, (5) update shared-context/THESIS.md if priorities have shifted. Push changes to repo via pr-workflow skill.

Check VolSync ReplicationSource status across all clusters. Verify last successful backup timestamps are within expected windows. Report any ReplicationSources with failed or stale conditions. Run: kubectl get replicationsource -A -o wide for each cluster context.

Check the Ottawa media stack health. For each service in the media namespace (sonarr-1080p, sonarr-4k, sonarr-anime, radarr-1080p, radarr-4k, radarr-4kremux, radarr-anime, prowlarr, lidarr, bazarr-1080p, bazarr-4k, bazarr-4kremux, bazarr-anime, jellyfin, plex, qbittorrent, qbittorrent-pvt, sabnzbd, autobrr, jellyseerr, overseerr, tautulli, jellystat, feedcord, wizarr, configarr): verify pod is Running and not restarting. For download clients (qbittorrent, qbittorrent-pvt, sabnzbd), check logs for connection errors. Check PVC usage for any media-related PVCs approaching capacity using: kubectl exec into any pod with df or use kubectl get pv and check capacity vs usage. Report failures to Discord only if something is actually broken - do NOT report if everything is healthy.

Audit Tailscale networking across all clusters. For each cluster context (ottawa, robbinsdale, stpetersburg): (1) Check tailscale operator pod health in tailscale-system namespace. (2) Verify Connectors are in ConnectorCreated status: kubectl get connectors -A. (3) Verify ProxyGroups are in ProxyGroupReady status: kubectl get proxygroups -A. (4) Check Recorder status: kubectl get recorders -A. (5) Verify cross-cluster Kubernetes API access by running kubectl --context <ctx> get nodes for each context from the openclaw pod. (6) Check for any tailscale-related pods in CrashLoopBackOff or Error state. Report any degraded Tailscale resources or cross-cluster connectivity failures.

Check TLS certificate health across all clusters. For each context (ottawa, robbinsdale, stpetersburg): (1) Run kubectl get certificates -A and check for any not in Ready=True state. (2) Check certificate expiry dates - flag anything expiring within 30 days. (3) Check cert-manager pod health. (4) Look for any CertificateRequest resources stuck in pending. Only report to Discord if there are actual issues found.

Verify cross-cluster mesh connectivity. (1) For each cluster context (ottawa, robbinsdale, stpetersburg), run kubectl get nodes to confirm API server reachability. (2) Check egress services in tailscale-system: kubectl get egressservices -A - verify they have valid status. (3) Check DNSConfig status: kubectl get dnsconfig -A. (4) Verify the ProxyGroup replicas match desired count. (5) Check Tailscale operator logs for recent errors: kubectl logs -n tailscale-system deployment/operator --tail=50 --since=12h | grep -i error. Only report if connectivity is degraded.

Identify stale Kubernetes resources across all clusters. For each context (ottawa, robbinsdale, stpetersburg): (1) Find completed/failed Jobs older than 7 days: kubectl get jobs -A. (2) Find pods in Evicted or Error state: kubectl get pods -A --field-selector=status.phase=Failed. (3) Find CrashLoopBackOff pods that have been crashing for >24 hours. (4) Check for orphaned PVCs not bound to any pod. (5) Check for stuck VolSync jobs (running >24 hours with 0 completions). Report all findings with recommended cleanup actions. Do NOT delete anything automatically - just report what should be cleaned up.

Deep Ceph storage analysis for Ottawa and Robbinsdale clusters. For each cluster with rook-ceph: (1) Check CephCluster CR status: kubectl get cephcluster -n rook-ceph -o yaml. (2) Verify all OSDs are running: kubectl get pods -n rook-ceph -l app=rook-ceph-osd. (3) Check for any OSD pods in CrashLoopBackOff or pending state. (4) Get Ceph health details from toolbox if available: kubectl exec -n rook-ceph deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status. (5) Check PG health: kubectl exec -n rook-ceph deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph pg stat. (6) Check capacity: kubectl exec -n rook-ceph deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph df. (7) Look for slow OSD operations or blocked requests. Report degraded OSDs, unhealthy PGs, or capacity warnings (>75% usage).
